era_context: Early Bronze Age III. Fifth Dynasty Egypt (c. 2494–2345 BCE) maintains centralized authority. Early Dynastic III Mesopotamia (c. 2900–2334 BCE) continues with competing city-states. The Indus Valley Civilization (c. 2600–1900 BCE) is in its mature urban phase. This period predates the Akkadian Empire (c. 2334–2154 BCE) and the Old Babylonian period.
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified; no events are documented for this year.
historiographic_note: The year -2947 is not attested in any surviving source as a year of particular significance. No primary texts, inscriptions, or archaeological evidence specifically document events from this year. The historical record for -2947 is essentially empty. This is not a failure of research but an honest reflection of the state of documentation for the Early Bronze Age. Most years in this period are undocumented at the year-specific level; only regnal years, astronomical calculations, and archaeological stratigraphy provide approximate dating.
